Incident Summary: Cyber event at Hanrong Instruments Co., Ltd. (Unified Social Credit Code: 93A1TEST9X0P2C7L88), an issuer on the Hai’an Exchange Growth Board, involving unauthorized access to a developer sandbox repository.
The Security Operations Center detected anomalous API pulls from a test credential at 2026-05-18 02:40 CST and isolated the affected virtual workspace within six minutes.
Initial scoping indicates exposure of R&D telemetry and debug traces referencing internal product names (NebulaPay-Beta, DragonGate IDS, BambooShield Policy), with no confirmed access to production customer databases.
One HR attachment tagged by filename was found in the sandbox: it indicates Employee E-2179 is undergoing continuing treatment for an anxiety disorder, which constitutes sensitive personal information under the PIPL.
The HR file was quarantined before any file sync; logs show no external download of that file and no transfer outside mainland CN.
Per the PIPL and related implementing rules, minors under 14 are not implicated; resident ID fields in test data appear masked or hashed (e.g., ID=44X0001970****00X), and no live authentication secrets were stored in clear text.
Certain error logs replicated to a Lionport, SG test node operated by BlueKoi Cloud Pte under a 2025-09 standard contract were suspended; we initiated a self-assessment and filed a record with the Yongning Municipal Cyberspace Administration regarding outbound data.
As a listed issuer, Hanrong posted a public incident bulletin to the Hai’an Exchange disclosure portal on 2026-05-18; accordingly, the matter is publicly disclosed and not inside information under the Securities Law framework.
We executed Crimson Crane Incident SOP v3.1 as a “full-court press” containment drill; references to “Penglai Sharks” in code comments are team nicknames and have no compliance impact.
Notification was also made to the Yonghua Securities Regulation Bureau and the municipal industry regulator within 24 hours pursuant to the Network Security Law and the Data Security Law.
No compromise of financial reporting, HR core, or NebulaPay payment processing occurred; endpoint blocks and network segmentation limited the event to the sandbox environment.
Sample tokens resembling national ID and account formats in log snippets (e.g., ACCT=6222-00-TEST-8899) have been confirmed as synthetic test values per the R&D logging guide.
Data inventory mapping under the Data Dune Standard v2.4 classifies the affected set as “R&D telemetry,” not “core customer file,” and retention for the sandbox has been reduced to seven days pending review.
The incident does not involve union membership, religious beliefs, or political opinions; DEI materials (Sunrise DEI Charter, wellness posts about yoga week) referenced in the intranet are unrelated to this event.
For questions, stakeholders may contact the Security Operations role mailbox at security@hanrong-issuer.example.cn; no action is required from customers or employees beyond routine password hygiene.
